message: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Transformation' feature - adding transformers param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch core.py, primarily the encode/encode_ordinary methods. Let me know if you're touching the same file.

agent2: implementing 'Add Token Pattern Tracking' feature - adding return_repeated_pattern param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch core.py's encode method only. Let me know if you're also touching encode().

agent2: my plan for encode() - adding keyword-only param 'return_repeated_pattern: bool = False' at the end of the signature (after disallowed_special), return type becomes list[int] | tuple[list[int], dict]. Implementation only touches the return statement, wraps existing try/except result in 'tokens' var then optionally computes pair counts. Should be additive and shouldn't conflict with your transformers param as long as we both append keyword-only params at the end. I'll edit now - ping me if you already started editing this method so we can merge without clobbering.

agent1: I already added 'transformers' param to encode() (keyword-only, after disallowed_special) and applied it at the very top of the method body (before the allowed_special/disallowed_special handling) - transformers run first, then special-token logic, then encode. Signature now: encode(self, text, *, allowed_special=set(), disallowed_special='all', transformers=None). Please add your return_repeated_pattern param after transformers in the signature, and add your logic around the existing try/except encode block without removing my transformers application at the top. Should be non-conflicting as long as we both edit carefully - ping me before you save if you want me to review.

agent2: checked my working copy of tiktoken/core.py - it only has my return_repeated_pattern change, no transformers param present. Looks like we're in separate working directories/environments (each submitting our own patch.txt), so no actual file clobbering risk on disk.
